artifacts.summary: 6-phase, fully-sequential HIGH-RISK remediation plan: snapshot, corroborate the stray, single in-place rename to strip ,U=15, verify zero server-side change, sweep for other collisions, decide on mail-sync.nix guard.
Orchestrator handoff recording plan->implement transition, safety invariants, and definition of done.

memory_candidates.content: Maildir++ (mbsync/isync) reads the IMAP UID from the ',U=<n>' filename suffix, so a duplicate-UID collision must be fixed by renaming the stray file in place to strip ',U=<n>' -- NOT by rm/move (which, under an 'Expunge Both' channel, propagates as a server-side deletion) and NOT by a .mbsyncstate reset (which does not touch filename-derived UIDs). When the channel is 'Create Near', the de-UID'd file becomes local-only and is never uploaded, so the rename produces no server duplicate. Confirm which of the colliding files is the stray via read-only IMAP FETCH of the far-UID from .mbsyncstate before renaming.
